title: Hyphen and dash
description: Hyphen (-) joins words, dash (—) sets off ideas. Both use the minus key.
text: well-known truth: write well-formed sentences. fast-paced work needs hand-eye care. so-so days happen.
finger_focus: Right pinky reaches up to minus / hyphen (no Shift)
tips: Hyphen (-) is right pinky, top row, just left of equals
Used for compound adjectives: well-known, fast-paced
No spaces around hyphens in compounds: well-known, NOT well - known

character_tips.anna: Hyphens glue words. No spaces around them in compounds.
character_tips.maxim: Right pinky stretches to minus key. Same key, no shift for hyphen.
character_tips.klavochka: Little dashes link words gently — pretty! 💖
